title: Affordable centimeter-scale 3D microscopy with submicrometer resolution - Nature Biotechnology
gist: A subscription-teaser abstract for HySIL (hybrid solid-liquid optics), a refractive framework with index-matched components that enables submicrometer-resolution 3D imaging of centimeter-scale samples using inexpensive air objectives instead of costly immersion objectives.

insights.claim: Hybrid solid-liquid optics (HySIL) use index-matched refractive components to deliver submicrometer-resolution 3D imaging of centimeter-scale samples across various immersion media while relying on inexpensive air objectives rather than costly immersion objectives.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: Cheaper-than-immersion-objective high-res 3D imaging could lower the cost base for tissue-clearing and pathology imaging workflows; watch instrumentation vendors and cleared-tissue imaging startups.
insights.quote: We developed hybrid solid–liquid optics (HySIL) — a refractive framework with index-matched components — for submicrometer-resolution 3D imaging of centimeter-scale samples in various immersion media using inexpensive air objectives.

insights.claim: Submicrometer-resolution 3D imaging of large samples has historically been constrained by the short working distance, high cost and inflexible design of immersion objectives, defining the bottleneck HySIL targets.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: Identifies the cost/working-distance constraint as the addressable pain point in large-sample microscopy.
insights.quote: Submicrometer-resolution three-dimensional (3D) imaging of large samples has been constrained by the short working distance, high cost and inflexible design of immersion objectives.
